symptoms.symptom_name: High fever with severe headache unresponsive to paracetamol

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic constitutional symptom of CNS infection.
Indicates CNS parenchymal involvement or raised intracranial pressure.
Absence does not exclude the diagnosis and must not be used for false reassurance.
Objective confirmation of meningeal irritation; clinician-only findings sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Fever, headache, neck stiffness/photophobia, and new confusion together confirm suspected bacterial meningitis with encephalitic features requiring immediate emergency antibiotics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate empirical IV antibiotics (and often IV dexamethasone) as early as possible, without delaying for confirmatory testing
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Every hour of delay in antibiotics significantly worsens outcomes in bacterial meningitis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent CT head and lumbar puncture in hospital
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms diagnosis and identifies the causative organism, but should not delay antibiotic administration.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Waiting to see if a rash develops before seeking emergency care
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Rash absence does not exclude bacterial meningitis; waiting risks catastrophic treatment delay.
